key_insight: The paper gives a unified convergence framework for decentralized stochastic subgradient methods under compressed communication, even for nonsmooth nonconvex objectives without Clarke regularity.
engineering_relevance: It provides practical algorithmic guidance for distributed learning systems that must trade off communication cost, robustness, and optimization accuracy.
method: Unifies decentralized stochastic subgradient-type methods with unbiased compression and contractive compression plus error compensation.
Tracks both consensus-error iterates and averaged iterates via continuous-time differential inclusions to prove global convergence.
Develops new compression-based variants, including sign-regularized methods and gradient-tracking momentum.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports preliminary numerical experiments that empirically support the theory and illustrate the communication-accuracy trade-off.
watch_item: The results are based on preliminary experiments, so the performance and tuning burden of the new compressed methods may vary in larger or more heterogeneous deployments.
paper_title: Decentralized Stochastic Subgradient-type Methods with Communication Compression for Nonsmooth Nonconvex Optimization
